Number of solutions to a+b+c=24 with a>=1, b,c>=0, each <=9. Let a'=a-1 => a'>=0, a'<=8. Then a'+b+c=23. Count nonnegative solutions with each <=9 (a'<=8, b<=9, c<=9). Count total solutions without upper bound: C(23+3-1,3-1)=C(25,2)=300. Subtract those where any variable exceeds limit. Use inclusion-exclusion. For a'>=9 (i.e., a'>=9 => a'>=9): let a''=a'-9 => a''>=0, then a''+b+c=14 => C(16,2)=120. For b>=10: b'=b-10 => b'+a'+c=13 => C(15,2)=105. For c>=10: same 105. Subtract sum: 120+105+105=330. Add back intersections: a'>=9 and b>=10: a''+b'+c=4 => C(6,2)=15. a'>=9 and c>=10: also 15. b>=10 and c>=10: b'+c'+a'=3 => C(5,2)=10. Sum intersections =15+15+10=40. Triple intersection a'>=9,b>=10,c>=10: a''+b'+c'= -? sum =23-9-10-10 = -6 impossible =>0. So total =300-330+40=10. So count=10. Probability =10/900=1/90. So answer correct.
